nameMeanings.name: Montgomery
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and northern Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Sainte-Foy-de-Montgomery and Saint-Germain-de-Montgomery (Calvados). In Ireland this surname was present in the medieval period, died out, and was then reintroduced from Scotland in the 17th century. It has been Gaelicized in Ireland as Mac Iomaire and in Scotland as Mac Gumaraid.

nameMeanings.name: Margery
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual medieval vernacular form of   Margaret   (now also commonly spelled   Marjorie  ). This form of the name is preserved in the nursery rhyme ‘See-saw, Margery Daw’.

historicEvents.title: The Act of Union
historicEvents.description: The Act of Union was a legislative agreement which united England, Scotland, Wales, and Ireland under the name of the United Kingdom on January 1, 1801.
The defeat of Napoleon Bonaparte at Waterloo marks the end of the Napoleonic Wars. Napoleon defeated and exiled to St. Helena.

historicEvents.title: The Factory Act Restricts Child Labor
historicEvents.description: The Factory Act restricted the hours women and children could work in textile mills. No child under the age of 9 were allowed to work, and children ages 9-13 could not work longer than 9 hours per day. Children up to the age of 13 were required to receive at least two hours of schooling, six days per week.

historicEvents.title: The Crimean War
historicEvents.description: The Crimean War was fought between Russia and an alliance of Britain, France, Sardinia and Turkey on the Crimean Peninsula. Russia had put pressure on Turkey which threatened British interests in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: School Attendance Becomes Mandatory for Children
historicEvents.description: School attendance became compulsory from ages five to ten on August 2, 1880.

historicEvents.title: The Entente Cordiale
historicEvents.description: The Entente Cordiale was signed between Britain and France on April 8, 1904, to reconcile imperial interests and pave the way for future diplomatic cooperation. This ended hundreds of years of conflict between the two states.

en.lifeSummary: Margery Montgomery was born in Derbyshire, England as the daughter of Nicholas Montgomery and Joan de Vernon. She had at least 6 sons and 1 daughter with John Curzon. She lived in Crich, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom in 1851.

fr.lifeSummary: Margery Montgomery est née à Derbyshire, Angleterre, fille de Nicholas Montgomery et Joan de Vernon. Elle a eu au moins 6 fils et 1 fille avec John Curzon. Elle a vécu à Crich, Derbyshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni en 1851.

de.lifeSummary: Margery Montgomery wurde in Derbyshire, England, als Tochter von Nicholas Montgomery und Joan de Vernon geboren. Sie hatte mit John Curzon mindestens 6 Söhne und 1 Tochter. 1851 hat sie in Crich, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom gelebt.

it.lifeSummary: Margery Montgomery è nata il nel Derbyshire, England come figlia di Nicholas Montgomery e Joan de Vernon. Lei avuto almeno 6 figli e 1 figlia con John Curzon. Vissuto a Crich, Derbyshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito nel 1851.

ja.lifeSummary: Margery MontgomeryはイギリスデルブイシュアイアでNicholas MontgomeryとJoan de Vernonの娘として生まれた。彼女はJohn Curzonとの間に少なくとも6人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1851年にはイギリスイギリスデルブイシュアイアクリチに住んでいた。

ko.lifeSummary: Margery Montgomery(은)는 영국 더비실르에서 Nicholas Montgomery(와)과 Joan de Vernon의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 John Curzon(와)과 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1851년도에 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르 크리치에서 살았다.

pt.lifeSummary: Margery Montgomery nasceu em Derbyshire, Inglaterra filha de Nicholas Montgomery e Joan de Vernon. Ela teve pelo menos 6 filhos e 1 filha com John Curzon. Ela viveu em Crich, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom em 1851.

ru.lifeSummary: Margery Montgomery родилась в местности дирбишир, Англия. Её родителей звали Nicholas Montgomery и Joan de Vernon. У неё было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 1 дочь от John Curzon. Она жила в местности крич, дирбишир, Англия, Великобритания в 1851 году.

es.lifeSummary: Margery Montgomery nació en Derbyshire, Inglaterra como hija de Nicholas Montgomery y Joan de Vernon. Tuvo por lo menos 6 hijos y 1 hija con John Curzon. Vivió en Crich, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido en 1851.
